refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation end-to-end communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the latency metric and its impact on safe teleoperation, clarifying the performance requirement and safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under controlled network conditions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms; conduct safety validation with teleoperation scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission under agreed network conditions.
FR3: Provide timely feedback to the operator to maintain safe control.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize network protocol stack and data compression to reduce latency.
DP2: Use dedicated low-latency communication channels with QoS guarantees.
DP3: Implement edge computing nodes to process data closer to operator and vehicle.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Use dedicated low-latency communication channels with QoS guarantees.

potential_sources: Dependency on network infrastructure and QoS enforcement.
Interaction with Safety Services for alerting on latency breaches.
coupling_check.notes: DP2 logically isolates latency control to communication channel design, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions can be controlled or guaranteed as per agreement.
Risk of network degradation impacting latency beyond design assumptions.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding fallback mechanisms or latency monitoring to mitigate network risks.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing teleoperation communication, including network link management and latency control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data with latency constraints.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time vehicle state data to the operator.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator commands for vehicle control.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on communication latency specifics.
Physical vehicle module; latency control is indirect.
Highest containment score; directly responsible for teleoperation communication.
Supports safety alerts but not primary latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency control is best contained within the Teleoperation module to centralize communication management and minimize diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential latency-related safety alerts may require close coordination with Safety Services.
Changes in network infrastructure may require cross-module updates.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish clear interface contracts with Safety Services for latency breach alerts and monitoring.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (dedicated low-latency communication channels) directly addresses the refined FR sub-requirements with clear assumptions and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if latency monitoring and alerting are not well coordinated with Safety Services.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module has strong containment for latency control, minimizing diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring latency on the Teleoperation module interfaces under test conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency control responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit latency breach alert interface with Safety Services.
Add fallback or monitoring mechanisms to handle network degradation.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition level is complete with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. All gates pass with manageable risks and clear verification paths.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission under agreed network conditions.
FR3: Provide timely feedback to the operator to maintain safe control.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network conditions can be controlled or guaranteed as per agreement.
Latency monitoring and alerting mechanisms will be implemented.
open_risks: Network degradation impacting latency beyond design assumptions.
Coordination between Teleoperation and Safety Services for latency alerts.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement data under agreed network conditions.
Verification of alerting mechanisms for latency breaches.
